gift-guide.title: Berlin Gift Guide - Quick Summary
items: **Ampelmann products** are Berlin’s most iconic modern souvenirs.
**Chocolate, pralines, and honey** are some of the safest Berlin gifts.
**Museum shops and Berliner Dom** are better for tasteful cultural gifts.
**Hackescher Markt and Hackesche Höfe** are best for stylish browsing.
**The best gifts are small and useful** - not bulky tourist junk.

pergamon-closed.title: Pergamon Museum Closed - Quick Summary
items: **Completely closed** since October 2023 - you cannot enter the main building.
The **Ishtar Gate** won't be visible again until the **2030s**.
**Das Panorama** is open nearby - 360° immersive experience, €14.
**Nefertiti is NOT in Pergamon** - she's in the Neues Museum, which is fully open.

berlin-food.title: Berlin Local Food Guide - Quick Summary
items: **Currywurst** is Berlin's most iconic street food - a grilled pork sausage sliced and drenched in spiced ketchup and curry powder. Budget €3-4 and eat it standing up like a local.
**Döner Kebab** was reinvented in Berlin - the city's Turkish community transformed it into a full meal. Berlin's version is genuinely different from anywhere else in the world.

**Bread culture runs deep** - Germany has over 300 bread varieties and Berlin bakeries take it seriously. A fresh Laugenbrezel (pretzel) or Schrippe (bread roll) with butter is a proper Berlin breakfast.
**Hackescher Markt** - the final stop on our walking tour - is surrounded by excellent food options, from traditional beer gardens to weekend street food markets.
**Most iconic Berlin foods cost under €5** - the best eating experiences happen at street stalls and market stands. Skip the tourist traps around Alexanderplatz.
